name: Moravian Christmas Loaf
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald 2 cups of the milk and let cool.
2. Dissolve yeast cake in 1/4 cup warm water.
3. Add dissolved yeast to the cooled scalded milk, flour, and salt. Mix well.
4. Cover and set aside to rise in a warm place overnight.
5. In the morning, scald another cup of milk and add melted butter. Combine with the yeast mixture.
6. Add sugar and remaining flour, kneading until no longer sticky. Use more flour if necessary.
7. Add chopped fruit and sprinkle with additional flour. Mix well.
8. Cover and let rise again until doubled in bulk.
9. Shape into small loaves, place in greased pans, and cover with sliced almonds.
10. Let rise for 2 hours.
11. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 40 minutes.
Source: Pennsylvania Dutch Cook Book - Fine Old Recipes, Culinary Arts Press, 1936.
